JavaScript tests for Number object creation, properties, methods, and prototype behavior.


## Files
- **[15.07.02-001.js](15.07.02-001.js.md)**: Tests the creation of a Number object from a string and verifies its type and value.
- **[15.07.02-002.js](15.07.02-002.js.md)**: Tests the creation of a Number object and its comparison to +0.0 in JavaScript.
- **[15.07.02-003.js](15.07.02-003.js.md)**: Tests the creation of a Number object with scientific notation and checks its type and value.
- **[15.07.02-004.js](15.07.02-004.js.md)**: Tests the conversion of a large number to a string in base 35 using the Number object.
- **[15.07.02-005.js](15.07.02-005.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of the Number object when modified and used with an Error object.
- **[15.07.02-006.js](15.07.02-006.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of JavaScript Number objects and their properties.
- **[15.07.02-007.js](15.07.02-007.js.md)**: Tests the behavior of the `toString` method on `Number` objects after deletion from the prototype.
- **[15.07.02-008.js](15.07.02-008.js.md)**: Tests if a Number object created with null has Number.prototype as its prototype.
- **[15.07.02-009.js](15.07.02-009.js.md)**: Tests if a Number object created with null has the correct prototype.
- **[15.07.02-010.js](15.07.02-010.js.md)**: Tests the constructor property of a Number object in JavaScript.
- **[15.07.02-011.js](15.07.02-011.js.md)**: Tests the default value of a newly created Number object in JavaScript.
